evals.expected_output: Tests calculateInvoiceTotal's happy path, edge cases, and tax override; explicitly skips fetchInvoice since it requires the API client.
expectations: Writes tests for calculateInvoiceTotal covering the happy path, an empty items array, and the taxRate override
Uses toBeCloseTo (not toBe) for any assertion involving the tax-rate multiplication, since it's a floating-point calculation
Explicitly states it is skipping fetchInvoice because it requires the API client, rather than silently ignoring it or trying to mock the client
Places the test file adjacent to the source (invoiceUtils.test.ts)

evals.prompt: Write unit tests for invoiceUtils.ts — there's already a test file for it, please check first.
evals.expected_output: Reads the existing invoiceUtils.test.ts, sees the happy-path case is already covered, and adds new describe/it blocks for edge cases rather than duplicating or overwriting the existing test.
expectations: Reads the existing invoiceUtils.test.ts before writing anything new
Does not duplicate the already-covered happy-path case for calculateInvoiceTotal with no tax
Adds new test cases (e.g. empty array, tax override) to the existing file rather than creating a second test file
